# Algorithm Myth Corrections

## Myth 1: Hashtags are the main growth lever

Correction:

```text
Hashtags help classification. They are not the main growth lever.
```

Tactic:

```text
Use relevant hashtags.
Avoid #FYP spam as strategy.
Put the actual topic in speech, text, and caption.
```

## Myth 2: Views alone tell you what worked

Correction:

```text
Views are a starting metric, not a quality metric.
```

For Shorts, public views became less diagnostic after the March 2025 view-count change. Track engaged views, chose-to-view, average percentage viewed, and subscriber conversion.

## Myth 3: More cuts always means more retention

Correction:

```text
Cuts only help when they create semantic change.
```

Good cuts:

```text
new example
new proof
new step
new comparison
new result
new emotional beat
```

Bad cuts:

```text
random zoom
random stock footage
pointless sound effect
motion without meaning
```

## Myth 4: Clickbait is a shortcut

Correction:

```text
Clickbait can increase starts but damage completion, trust, and follow conversion.
```

Tactic:

```text
Use high-tension accurate hooks.
Never create a gap the video cannot close.
```

## Myth 5: Shorter is always better

Correction:

```text
Better = enough length to sustain tension, proof, and payoff.
```

Tactic:

```text
Test idea density, not length alone.
```

## Myth 6: Cross-posting identical videos is efficient

Correction:

```text
The same idea needs platform-native grammar.
```

Tactic:

```text
TikTok: search/culture/comment loop
Reels: sendability/identity/Trial Reels
Shorts: chose-to-view/search/subscriber conversion
```
